perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may not be assigned by either party without the prior written consent of the other party.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may not be assigned by the Company without the prior written consent of Cano. Cano may assign this agreement at its sole discretion.
perturbation.explanation: The original agreement states that neither party can assign the agreement without written consent. The modification allows Cano to assign the agreement at its sole discretion, but restricts the company from assigning the agreement in any instance, thus directly contradicting the first sentence of the clause and creating an imbalance in rights.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Assignment. This Agreement may not be assigned by the Company without the prior written consent of Cano. Cano may assign this agreement at its sole discretion.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that neither party can assign the agreement without prior written consent. The changed text modifies this by allowing Cano to assign the agreement at its sole discretion while restricting the Company from assigning the agreement at all without consent. This directly contradicts the initial agreement where both parties were subject to the same restrictions and creates an imbalance in rights. The contradiction is evident by comparing the original text and the changed text. The contradicted text is the same as the Changed text.

perturbation.original_text: In consideration of the services to be rendered under this Agreement as set forth above, the Company shall be entitled to compensation in the amount of $150,000.00 (One Hundred Fifty Thousand Dollars), to be paid in equal installments of $37,500.00 (Thirty Seven Thousand Five Hundred Dollars) on January 1, 2008, April 1, 2008, July 1, 2008 and October 1, 2008. If Cano fails to make any payment when required by this Agreement, the Company shall have the option of canceling this Agreement following the passage of ten (10) days after having given Cano written notice of its default. While the Company shall have no further obligation to Cano following cancellation of the Agreement, Cano shall remain liable for all unpaid installments for the then current term of the Agreement, which installments shall be immediately due and payable.
perturbation.changed_text: In consideration of the services to be rendered under this Agreement as set forth above, the Company shall be entitled to compensation in the amount of $150,000.00 (One Hundred Fifty Thousand Dollars), to be paid in equal installments of $37,500.00 (Thirty Seven Thousand Five Hundred Dollars) on January 1, 2008, April 1, 2008, July 1, 2008 and October 1, 2008, unless Cano determines that the services provided are not satisfactory. If Cano fails to make any payment when required by this Agreement, the Company shall have the option of canceling this Agreement following the passage of ten (10) days after having given Cano written notice of its default. While the Company shall have no further obligation to Cano following cancellation of the Agreement, Cano shall remain liable for all unpaid installments for the then current term of the Agreement, which installments shall be immediately due and payable.
perturbation.explanation: The original text specifies the payment schedule as a requirement. The added phrase 'unless Cano determines that the services provided are not satisfactory' introduces an element of subjectivity. This creates a contradiction because it gives Cano the power to withhold payments based on its subjective satisfaction, conflicting with the initial, seemingly unconditional, payment obligation. This contradiction could make the clause difficult to enforce as it creates uncertainty in when the payment should be made.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Payment of Money. In consideration of the services to be rendered under this Agreement as set forth above, the Company shall be entitled to compensation in the amount of $150,000.00 (One Hundred Fifty Thousand Dollars), to be paid in equal installments of $37,500.00 (Thirty Seven Thousand Five Hundred Dollars) on January 1, 2008, April 1, 2008, July 1, 2008 and October 1, 2008, unless Cano determines that the services provided are not satisfactory. If Cano fails to make any payment when required by this Agreement, the Company shall have the option of canceling this Agreement following the passage of ten (10) days after having given Cano written notice of its default. While the Company shall have no further obligation to Cano following cancellation of the Agreement, Cano shall remain liable for all unpaid installments for the then current term of the Agreement, which installments shall be immediately due and payable.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a condition ('unless Cano determines that the services provided are not satisfactory') that allows Cano to withhold payment based on subjective criteria. This directly contradicts the initial statement that the Company 'shall be entitled to compensation' according to a fixed schedule. The original text implies an unconditional right to payment, while the modified text makes that right conditional on Cano's satisfaction, creating a direct conflict regarding the payment obligation. The Contradicted Text is in the same location as the Changed Text, but the change introduces a contradiction with the initial payment schedule.
